feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite relation to SAP Extensibility guideline
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

There's is a training planned within our team regarding SAP Extensibility guideline template. 

We are working on PO to CI migration, How can we make use of SAP Extensibility guideline template for SAP Integration Suite? Can SAP Extensibility be used for redesign of Proxy/Flat file interfaces? Could you please let me know?  Accordingly, I could ask my trainer in the training session planned. Thank you! 

Regards, 

Ramu.

entry.title: In API Proxy, can I change All HTTP method to 'HEAD' before forwarding to Target Endpoint?
entry.content:
Hi,

I use API Proxy in Integration Suite, and here is what I try to implement:

I want to check health status of this API(to confirm if it is available):
https://cpi-mmt-dev.it-cpi018-rt.cfapps.eu10-003.hana.ondemand.com/http/** (all path under /http/.*, like /http/operation/error_iflows)
so I only need to call it with HTTP HEAD method - I don't need any payload since I only need to know if it returns 200.

I create a API Proxy URL: https://maco-dev.test.apimanagement.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/health.

 

No matter what HTTP method(GET, POST, ...) calls this Proxy URL, the Proxy Endpoint will forward this request to Target Endpoint with method HEAD. I.e, all request from client will be replaced with HTTP method 'HEAD'.

I tried to achieve by setting values of request.method in javascript:

I tried three ways:

However, even if method set to 'HEAD'(use `request.method='HEAD'`, another two ways above can't change), the Target Endpoint still return 200 but with payload:

 

Is there ideas for this topic? thanks in advance!😆

entry.title: BRIM Integration/ Strategical relevance of BTP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi folks,

Today I got into trouble with a customer who we have sold Integration Suite as THE strategic platform for his enterprise integration needs. The customer already uses DI for data integration and is super SAP heavy/centric. Now one of the first new projects is introducing SAP BRIM. And I had to learn that SAP proposed to our customer not to use integration suite but instead to use convergent mediation (https://www.sap.com/germany/products/financial-management/convergent-mediation.html) which I did not even know about before.

My customer is furious that we proposed integration suite and now they need another integration product in their first project. Also I saw some BRIM standard content on the business hub, but at the same time convergent mediation seems to be the tool of choice. Is there anybody out there who already implemented BRIM and had to decide between the two integration solutions? Is IS really not suited equally?

This case also makes me wonder why sap would not go for an integration suite first approach for such cases. With the ERP getting more and more modular I am afraid that we will get more such cases where micro solutions get their own integration solution bundled. That would make IS with its recent price raise a more difficult sell since the strategic importance could be questioned.

 Would be interesting to get the communities take on this and some kind of guidance would be highly appreciated.

Thanks,

Peter

 

SAP Integration Strategy SAP Integration Suite @UdoPaltzer @svenhuberti

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing - Customization for Messages without Integration Workflow Configuration
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

For the message “Inspection Results Recording” there is no Workflow Configuration and we have following problem:

These are the CI Artifacts triggered from this message.

I need to send the SFC as BAPI-Parameter EXT_NO (Single-Unit Number for the Unit to Be Inspected):

What is the best way to make this kind of customization?

In this case, there is no Business Rule available (only for automatic collections) and the POD has no button that allows me to trigger a Production Process Designer when the manual inpection lot collection happens. 

Is it possible to trigger a customized IFlow for this message?

Thanks in advance

Gabriela

entry.title: S/4 HANA API_SALES_ORDER_SIMULATION_SRV get JSON instead of XML
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm building a flow on BTP Integration Suite to call API_SALES_ORDER_SIMULATION_SRV OData from S/4.

I'm using OData Adapter, the flow works fine and I receive a response from S/4.

However, the response is in XML format, and I need OData Json format.

I tried adding content modifiers as "Accept: application/json", but still I get an XML.

How can I achieve that? Anyone has a working example?

Thanks,

Adriano

entry.title: Agreement Template not loading the creation page
entry.content: After Company profile and Trading partner setup I am trying to create a agreement template in my trial account. But the button I am clicking and it is not loading the next page to create the template.

entry.title: Integration Suite: Problem with Message Mapping in nested structures with the Groovy-Script
entry.content:
Hello dear experts,

I am trying to access nested values ​​in the GrovysScript using certain conditions in an Iflow as part of a message mapping in the Integration Suite, but it is not working and I have no idea how to solve the problem.

The following case or structure is present:

 

 

 

<ParameterValues>
	<ParameterCode>001</ParameterCode>
	<FieldListValue>
		<FieldListCode>1552</FieldListCode>
		<DescriptionValue>
			<LanguageCode>E</LanguageCode>
			<Description>123456</Description>
		</DescriptionValue>
	</FieldListValue>
	<Sequence>0</Sequence>
</ParameterValues>

<ParameterValues>
	<ParameterCode>002</ParameterCode>
	<FieldListValue>
		<FieldListCode>1223</FieldListCode>
		<DescriptionValue>
			<LanguageCode>E</LanguageCode>
			<Description>9881</Description>
		</DescriptionValue>
	</FieldListValue>
	<Sequence>0</Sequence>
</ParameterValues>

<ParameterValues>
	<ParameterCode>003</ParameterCode>
	<FieldListValue>
		<FieldListCode>4235</FieldListCode>
		<DescriptionValue>
			<LanguageCode>E</LanguageCode>
			<Description>34598</Description>
		</DescriptionValue>
	</FieldListValue>
	<Sequence>0</Sequence>
</ParameterValues>

 

 

 

When using message mapping in an array, I want to access an element <ParameterValues> which has the code 003 in the child node <ParameterCode>.

If the node has been found, the value 34598 from the node <Description> should be returned in the child node <DescriptionValue>. The structure I am mapping to is not nested but flat.

This is what the mapping for the fields looks like:

The getValue() function in Groovy-Script looks like this:

 

 

 

def void getValue(String[] parameterValues, String[] descriptionValues, Output value, MappingContext context) {
	parameterValues.each{ parameterValue ->
		switch (parameterValue.ParameterCode) {
			case '003':
			    value.addValue(descriptionValues[0].Description);
			break
		}
	}
}

 

 

 

Which step is still missing or can I access the individual nodes so easily in the script?

Best regards
Arthur

entry.title: Integration of SAP PI-PP or SAP PI-PCS with Aveva MES
entry.content:
Hi Team,


I just have a quick question, is there any possibility to communicate SAP- PI-PCS with Aveva MES system for the process industry. I want to transfer the manufacturing process values and parameters in addition wants to transfer plant data, production process quality management, order tracking etc from SAP to MES.

I know there is SAP PP module and it has SAP PP-PCS interface to link with the external 3rd parties software. Can anyone help me to figure out the way how can I connect or integrate the SAP PP-PI with Aveva MES.

Any input would be appreciated.

Thanks & Best Regards

SSamber

entry.title: OData Upsert Response in French as opposed to English
entry.content:
Hi All, 

I am trying to investigate if we can dictate the language in which SuccessFactors returns a response to CPI. 

I am currently working on an interface where I fetch data from SF via a GET call using the Odata v2 adapter. The response received in this case is in English.

However, when I upsert with a POST call via an OData v2 adapter. I get the response in French. 

I tried testing this via Postman and in this scenario, via an HTTP call, I got the response in English. The same with CPI when I change the adapter from HTTP to Odata v2, I get the response back in English.

With the Odata v2 adapter, I tried sending the headers: Accept-Language: en also sap-language en but I still got the response back in French. Has anyone experienced this or found a way to resolve it?

Any input is appreciated. 

Response Received from CPI above

Response received from Postman.

Regards,

Amrit

entry.title: Threshold for data store in Cloud Integration
entry.content: Our integration package published in API Hub contains integration flows that uses data store. For instance in one of the data stores we are saving the absence records in order to determine based on the unique external ID if a particular absence record has been updated in SuccessFactors Employee Central. As we are dealing with customers having huge population the demand to keep all the absence records in anticipation of any updates to them raises a potential concern with the data store collapsing or failing due to large amount of data. Though we have a tool to perform clean up of the data store we would like to know the limit of the data store like threshold in terms of file size so that we can make a decision in terms of how to manage it. Also, any advise to deal with our case is much appreciated as we need to at least keep all the absence records in the data store for at least a year.

entry.title: Zipping multiple files from SFTP to SFTP in one folder
entry.content:
Hi

I've a scenario where I want to pick multiple .txt files from a source SFTP and zip it in SAP CPI and place it in target SFTP folder. 

The names of the files should not be changed, and all the files have to be zipped in one folder and placed in target SFTP.

 

This is my flow.

Reading file name : The camel expression is ${header.CamelFileName}

Gather Step:

 

So far, when I process the data only one file is getting zipped, for example if I have 4 files in the folder I want all four to be zipped in one folder, but only one is getting zipped. 

Please help me with the solution.

Thanks and Regards.

Shreyas

entry.title: What are the best practices for accessing files on an SAP S/4HANA system from SAP Cloud Integration?
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Variations of this question have been asked before in the Community.  However, I would like to be more specific and request your feedback based on latest experiences.

Context
There are scenarios where due to legacy system requirements, files must be output from an SAP S/4HANA system and integrated with the legacy system via SAP Integration Suite (SAP Cloud Integration).  In my case, the system is SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition.

The classic approach is for files to be output to an AL11 directory, in which case SAP Note 3343395 - FAQ: How to connect Cloud Integration to T-code AL11 recommends:

"Cloud Integration cannot directly connect to T-code AL11.
Your System Administrator / Integration Developer should set up FTP or SFTP service, for Cloud Integration to consume the file repository."

Solution Options
The following proposals have been suggested to address the requirement:

1.  Set up / co-deploy an SFTP server directly on the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Server.

2.  Store the file object in the SAP TemSe Data Storage and expose an OData API that reads Temse data storage so that it can be consumed by SAP Cloud Integration to access the file object, thereby avoiding the need for setting up an SFTP server.

3.  Wait for the SAP Integration Edge Cell File Adapter planned for Q1 2025.

I would very much appreciate your experiences and recommendations on this topic.

Thanks,

Mustafa.

entry.title: Absent type deduction
entry.content:
I have created the absent type but the deduction did not occur when running payroll. I need guidance on this matter. Can someone help me with this?

Thanks in Advance

Kaira

entry.title: Message size limit in sap cloud integration
entry.content:
Hi,

There is always a debate between cloud integration vs ETL tool and when to use what especially when an org has both but managed by different teams.

One of the important factor is message size. However I have not come across any blog or piece of information that mentions the message size limit in cloud integration for each transaction. Can someone share the same?

Also if there are some points of considerations to make for CI vs ETL

@finny_babu @Sriprasadsbhat 

Best Regards

entry.title: JSON fromat Error
entry.content:
Hi Team,
Expected Json format is like Below ( this format is when i run in the postman showing successful) 

 
{
    "sequenceNumber": ,
    "uhlId": " ",
    "dataType": " ",
    "plantCodeLetter": " ",
    "plantIdentifier": " ",
    "fileCreationDate": " ",
    "fileCreationTime": " ",
    "sourceSystem": " ",
    "programVersionOne": " ",
    "programVersionTwo": "string",
    "programVersionThree": "string",
    "tt971Date": " ",
    "tt971Time": " ",
    "completeDataLoad": " ",
    "payload": [ " "
]
} 
As per the expectation i have Created structure Like below

After run the interface also  Json format convertion also coming in SAP PO  like below
{"sequenceNumber": X,"uhlId":"XXX,"dataType":"XXXX","plantCodeLetter":"string","plantIdentifier":"XXX","fileCreationDate":20230127,"fileCreationTime":20230127,"sourceSystem":"XXXX","programVersionOne":"YYYYY","programVersionTwo":"string","programVersionThree":"string","tt971Date":20230127,"tt971Time":"00000","completeDataLoad":"00000","payload":[ " " ]
}
 looks like both are same, But when I use this json format in the Postman tool it is showing as 400 BAD request

Could you please let me know , what changes i have to do,

entry.title: Error while calling SAP API Proxy connected to SAP Gateway service
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

The client/consumer of our SAP API Proxy is facing the below issue while calling it.
FYI, API Proxy is connected to backend OData gateway service via API provider. While doing the $metadata call to the SAP gateway, it responded with below error to SAP API M. But after 4 hours the service was back and we see successful message.
But since this is an intermittent issue, we wanted to understand the root cause of the failure. Also where do we get the error logs in SAP gateway for GET $metadata calls that are failed.

Error:

{
  "fault": {
    "faultstring": "Unexpected EOF at target",
    "detail": {
      "errorcode": "messaging.adaptors.http.flow.UnexpectedEOFAtTarget"
    }
  }

Thanks,

Jovita

entry.title: SAP AEM use case : asynch outbound and inbound interface
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement to build asynchronous outbound and inbound interface with our SAP Backend.
Regarding asynchronous outbound interface(SAP Backend as sender) I know we can try to use AEM to push the events to partner system(receiver) REST API. How about asynchronous inbound scenario? Can we still use AEM to pull the files/data from partner system or what should partner system support? Should they publish to AMQP queues and then later use CI to pull the messages and use AEM later to push to SAP? (or) should we avoid AEM for inbound interface alone?

For outbound asynchronous interface we have to clarify with partner system how they want.
I understood that if AEM would like to push the data then they need to develop REST API on their side to consume. If partner system would like to pull the data from AEM queues - what is the setup needed from partner system side? Should they use AMQP protocol? Currently, the connectivity options offered by partner system is MQ, CFT, ESB etc., the question is what kind of technology they need to use on their side to pull the messages from AEM Queues? Kindly let me know the points that needs to be aligned with partner system and what kind of development efforts needed from their end(like building REST API) etc., and what kind of details/information needed to be gathered from them? Thank you!

Regards,

Ramu.

entry.title: Connect to On premise system without Cloud Connector
entry.content:
How we can connect with the on-premise system without using a cloud connector?

The on-premise system is located in DMZ, so the customer doesn't allow us to set up a Cloud Connector, they just provide IP and Port (CPI is added to the whitelist).

I have called directly but error.

The message is om.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occurred: connection timed out after 10000 ms: /45.1******:18***.
The MPL ID for the failed message is: AGaCFKrKQQkrmqf3qUd-TmSUcXG2

entry.title: BTP IS Open Connector - Request Body Prehook Script Help
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am in the process of creating the open connector the connection from BTP IS open connector. as per the API requirement I have to pass the below body in order get successful(POST API).  

{ "EmployeeStatus": "Active", "Firstname": "", "Lastname": ""}

Please guide/direct me on writing prehook script to pass the body Jason

Regards, Suresh

entry.title: How to Call an iFlow from SAP CAP Java?
entry.content:
I want to call an Integration Suite IFlow from the SAP CAP Java App.

and I am using BTP Destination for this.

So Basically, I have defined the endpoints of iFlow and creds in the Destination, binded the Destination with the deployed application.
I followed this blog: Consuming external OData service from CAP Java application

My Java Code:

 

package customer.integration_suite.handlers;

import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;
import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;

import com.sap.cds.services.handler.EventHandler;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.Destination;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.DestinationAccessor;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.HttpClientAccessor;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.exception.DestinationAccessException;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.exception.DestinationNotFoundException;

import java.io.BufferedReader;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.io.InputStreamReader;

import org.apache.http.HttpResponse;
import org.apache.http.client.HttpClient;
import org.apache.http.client.methods.HttpGet;

@RestController
public class IntegrationSuiteConnection  implements EventHandler {
    
    @GetMapping("/integration-suite")
    public String connectToIntegrationSuite() {
        String DESTINATION_NAME = "IntegrationSuite";

        try {
            Destination destination = DestinationAccessor.getDestination(DESTINATION_NAME);
            HttpClient client = HttpClientAccessor.getHttpClient(destination);
            HttpResponse httpResponse = null;

            try{
                httpResponse = client.execute(new HttpGet());
                if (httpResponse.getStatusLine().getStatusCode() == 200) {
                    BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(
                        new InputStreamReader(httpResponse.getEntity().getContent()));
                    StringBuilder result = new StringBuilder();
                    String line = null;
                    while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {
                        result.append(line);
                    }
                    reader.close();
                    return result.toString();
                }
            } catch (IOException e) {
                return "IOException: " + e.getMessage();
            }
        } catch (DestinationNotFoundException | DestinationAccessException e) {
            return e.getMessage();
        }

        return null;
    }
}

 

My iFlow is returning just a simple String.

But on hitting the url : https://81fff548trial-dev-integration-suite.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/integration-suite, i am not getting the desired result.
I am getting this on the browser: Failed to get destination.
Please help me to resolve this.

Also, I wanted to know to know, is this even the right way to call an iFlow from the CAP app?
Please let me know about this too.

Thanks,
Abhinav
